events.title: Snack & Study
events.description: Join us every Thursday for Snack & Study at AAS! From 1:00-4:00, the AAS building is open for student use. Pull up a seat in our cozy Reading Room and enjoy a quiet space where you can study, read, or just take a break from it all. Free hot drinks and snacks available while supplies last!

events.title: Beinecke Scholarship Information Session
events.location: 514 Illini Union Bookstore Building, 807 S Wright St Champaign IL, 61820
events.description: If you are a high-achieving junior and are planning to attend graduate school after graduation, but have concerns about being able to afford it, consider applying for the Beinecke Scholarship. The National and International Scholarships Program has planned sessions to assist students towards earning this $35,000 award. February 5th, 2026 we will be conducting an informational session, including a question and answer period with a previous recipient, from 3:30-4:30 p.m. and on February 6th a Beinecke workshop from 3:00-4:00 p.m. to help students get started on their actual applications.  Both sessions will be held in 514 IUB.The Beinecke Foundation is looking for students who have demonstrated superior standards of intellectual ability, scholastic achievement, and personal promise during their undergraduate years. The program seeks to encourage and enable highly motivated U.S. citizen juniors to pursue graduate studies in the arts, humanities, and social sciences. Each scholar receives $5,000 immediately prior to entering graduate school and an additional $30,000 while attending graduate school. Preference is given to candidates for whom the awarding of significant financial aid would increase the likelihood of the student's being able to attend graduate school. For more information, contact the scholarships office at topscholars@illinois.edu or visit our website at Beinecke – National & International Scholarships Program

events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Imagination, Faith, and Desire: Art and Agency in European Prints, 1475–1800
events.location: Krannert Art Museum, 500 E. Peabody Dr., Champaign
events.description: With over 100 prints on display—including works by Rembrandt, Goya, and Dürer—Imagination, Faith, and Desire is extraordinary for the significance of the works and the exceptionally high quality of the impressions, the excellence of which rivals the holdings at many major museums. On view September 25, 2025, through February 28, 2026. Curated by Maureen Warren, Curator of European and American Art, with contributions from Yajie Hui, Nancy Karrels, and Sandra Racek. In conjunction with the exhibition, Krannert Art Museum is hosting a daylong symposium: Envisioning Gender and Sexuality in Premodern European Prints (Oct 17). Open Tueday–Friday (10–5), Saturday (10–4), and Thursdays until 8 pm, when school is in session. The museum is free and open to everyone. Parking nearby is free after 5 pm and on weekends. This exhibition is supported by the Ursula and R. Stanley Johnson Charitable Fund; the Sandra L. Batzli Memorial Fund; DutchCultureUSA, a program of the Kingdom of the Netherlands in the United States; and in part by Villa Albertine—The French Institute for Culture and Education/Cultural Services of the French Embassy. Krannert Art Museum acknowledges support from the Illinois Arts Council.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: Beckman Visualization Lab Office Hours
events.description: Beckman’s Visualization Lab is open for office hours weekdays between 1-3 p.m. in 2203 Beckman, which is located just across the atrium bridge from the elevator. Manager Jay Cournoyer will be on hand to help users with the Vis Lab’s equipment and other resources for research. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? Email itg-vl@beckman.illinois.edu.

events.title: Eat the Rainbow
events.description: Nutrition Peers will be offering a variety of fruit to help keep students well nourished during their study hours at the Main Library. Learn about the importance of having a wide variety of colors in your diet and the nutrition benefits of fruit and vegetables. At this time, students are given the opportunity to connect with McKinley Health Center's Registered Dietitian and services provided at McKinley and through Nutrition Peers.

events.title: Multi-Species Justice Symposium: Paradigms, Practices, Possibilities
events.location: Levis Faculty Center, Room 210, 919 W. Illinois St
events.description: How does recognizing the fundamental entanglements of humans and the more-than-human world impact notions of "justice"? Drawing on perceptions from diverse communities, disciplines, and social, political, and historical contexts, this symposium will provide a space for us to grapple with the question: What might a more just world or worlds look like in the 21st century? Alex Blanchette (Anthropology, Tufts University) and Daina Bray (Yale Law Schoole) join an interdisciplinary group of scholars for a multi-day conversation about multi-species justice.

events.title: Snack & Study
events.description: Join us every Thursday for Snack & Study at AAS! From 1:00-4:00, the AAS building is open for student use. Pull up a seat in our cozy Reading Room and enjoy a quiet space where you can study, read, or just take a break from it all. Free hot drinks and snacks available while supplies last!

events.title: Voyager Scholarship Information Session
events.location: 514 Illini Union Bookstore Building, 807 S Wright St Champaign IL, 61820
events.description: The Voyager Scholarship (Obama-Chesky Scholarship for Public Service) provides up to $80,000 ($25,000/yr of financial aid for junior and senior year, $10,000 for a summer experience, and $2,000 annually for 10 years for travel) for students focused on public service careers. U.S. citizens, permanent residents, or DACA recipients who are entering their junior year with a 3.0+ GPA and demonstrated financial need are eligible. The National and International Scholarships Program is offering a Voyager Scholarship information session to provide interested applicants with an overview of the scholarship and an opportunity to hear tips from a scholarship recipient.  The info session will be held from 3:30-4:30 p.m. in 514 IUB. For more information, contact the scholarships office at topscholars@illinois.edu or visit our website at Voyager Scholarship – National & International Scholarships Program

events.description: Enjoy a free concert featuring the Music & Performing Arts Library’s Steinway Duo-Art Reproducing Piano! This event will take place in the Music & Performing Arts Library's player piano room, which is located on the second floor of the library. MPAL is located in the Music Building (1114 W Nevada St., Urbana). See our website for accessibility information and how to get to MPAL.

Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Imagination, Faith, and Desire: Art and Agency in European Prints, 1475–1800
events.location: Krannert Art Museum, 500 E. Peabody Dr., Champaign
events.description: With over 100 prints on display—including works by Rembrandt, Goya, and Dürer—Imagination, Faith, and Desire is extraordinary for the significance of the works and the exceptionally high quality of the impressions, the excellence of which rivals the holdings at many major museums. On view September 25, 2025, through February 28, 2026. Curated by Maureen Warren, Curator of European and American Art, with contributions from Yajie Hui, Nancy Karrels, and Sandra Racek. In conjunction with the exhibition, Krannert Art Museum is hosting a daylong symposium: Envisioning Gender and Sexuality in Premodern European Prints (Oct 17). Open Tueday–Friday (10–5), Saturday (10–4), and Thursdays until 8 pm, when school is in session. The museum is free and open to everyone. Parking nearby is free after 5 pm and on weekends. This exhibition is supported by the Ursula and R. Stanley Johnson Charitable Fund; the Sandra L. Batzli Memorial Fund; DutchCultureUSA, a program of the Kingdom of the Netherlands in the United States; and in part by Villa Albertine—The French Institute for Culture and Education/Cultural Services of the French Embassy. Krannert Art Museum acknowledges support from the Illinois Arts Council.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: Beckman Visualization Lab Office Hours
events.description: Beckman’s Visualization Lab is open for office hours weekdays between 1-3 p.m. in 2203 Beckman, which is located just across the atrium bridge from the elevator. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? events.title: Student Racial Justice Allies & Advocates (1.0)
events.description: The Racial Justice Allies and Advocates Training program is designed to provide students, faculty, and staff with a core awareness of the structures of racism, its role in interpersonal relationships, and empower allies to elevate the voices of underrepresented populations at the University of Illinois.

events.title: Spurlock Museum Book Club: An Indigenous Peoples’ History of the United States
events.location: The Literary, 122 N Neil St, Champaign, IL 61820
events.description: Spurlock Museum is proud to partner with The Literary (external link) to present this new community program taking place on several dates between June 2025 and April 2026. Free books will be provided to the first 13 people that sign up for participation for each date. Stop by the museum anytime during open hours to pick up your free book. Then join the group at the Literary in downtown Champaign on the designated discussion day. This book will be available for pickup at the Museum starting on December 1, 2025.

events.title: Unit for Criticism & Interpretive Theory Lecture: Carlos Alberto Torres, Distinguished Professor at UCLA, "Career of Paulo Freire, author of Pedagogy of the Oppressed"
events.description: Save the Date: Lecture on Paulo Freire On February 19, Carlos Alberto Torres, Distinguished Professor at UCLA and former Director of that university’s Latin American Center, will give a lecture celebrating the extraordinary humanitarian career of Paulo Freire, author of Pedagogy of the Oppressed, in which he analyzed the analogous relationship of colonizer and colonized to that of teacher and student. Please mark your calendars! Further details to follow. TIME/LOCATION TBD

events.title: Snack & Study
events.description: Join us every Thursday for Snack & Study at AAS! From 1:00-4:00, the AAS building is open for student use. Pull up a seat in our cozy Reading Room and enjoy a quiet space where you can study, read, or just take a break from it all. Free hot drinks and snacks available while supplies last!

events.title: Samvid Scholarship Information Session
events.description: The Samvid scholarship provides up to $100,000 to cover two years of funding leading to a graduate degree (MD, MBA, JD, MPP, MPH, EdD, or MS/MA in social sciences or STEM) at any U.S. university. Undergraduate seniors, graduate or professional students, and graduates are eligible to apply. U.S. citizens and DACA recipients are eligible.  The National and International Scholarships Program is offering a Samvid Scholarship information session to provide interested applicants with an overview of the scholarship and hear tips for creating a strong application. The info session will be held from 3:30-4:30 p.m. in 514 IUB For more information, contact the scholarships office at topscholars@illinois.edu or visit our website at www.topscholars.illinois.edu

events.description: With over 100 prints on display—including works by Rembrandt, Goya, and Dürer—Imagination, Faith, and Desire is extraordinary for the significance of the works and the exceptionally high quality of the impressions, the excellence of which rivals the holdings at many major museums. On view September 25, 2025, through February 28, 2026. Curated by Maureen Warren, Curator of European and American Art, with contributions from Yajie Hui, Nancy Karrels, and Sandra Racek. In conjunction with the exhibition, Krannert Art Museum is hosting a daylong symposium: Envisioning Gender and Sexuality in Premodern European Prints (Oct 17). Open Tueday–Friday (10–5), Saturday (10–4), and Thursdays until 8 pm, when school is in session. The museum is free and open to everyone. Parking nearby is free after 5 pm and on weekends. This exhibition is supported by the Ursula and R. Stanley Johnson Charitable Fund; the Sandra L. Batzli Memorial Fund; DutchCultureUSA, a program of the Kingdom of the Netherlands in the United States; and in part by Villa Albertine—The French Institute for Culture and Education/Cultural Services of the French Embassy. Krannert Art Museum acknowledges support from the Illinois Arts Council.

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Application Deadline - Graduate Concentration in Entrepreneurship and Innovation
events.description: Grainger Graduate Students - Do you want to be a leader in your field?  Do you have an innovative or entrepreneurial spirit, or aspire to commercialize technology? Do you want to understand the role of strategic innovation in industry-relevant problems?  The Entrepreneurship & Innovation Concentration will give you the skills, resources, and experiences necessary to become a successful innovator, entrepreneur, and leader who can account for both the technical and business facets of industry-relevant problems. Plus, completion of this concentration is officially recognized on your transcript. Applications are accepted year-round, but if you want to be eligible for Spring 2026, you must apply by March 1. Learn More and Apply

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.description: Since 2011, the Illinois Interfaith Planning Initiative has worked to create programs designed to invite interfaith engagement, understanding, and cooperation for the University of Illinois community. Regular events include a monthly “First Tuesday” interfaith dialogue.  All people of religious and non-religious philosophical traditions are welcome!  Questions? Contact Ann Rasmus at ann@universityymca.org.

events.title: Carr Visiting Author Series: Stephen Markley
events.description: A reading by Stephen Markley, made possible by the Robert J. and Katherin Carr visiting author series.  Stephen Markley is the author of The Deluge, hailed by The New York Times Book Review as an Editor's choice. His previous books include the critically acclaimed bestseller Ohio, as well as Publish this Book and Tales of Iceland. He has also written for the hulu comedy Only Murders in the Building. He is a graduate of the Iowa Writers workshop

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: 2026 Match Day
events.location: I Hotel & Illinois Conference Center - 1900 South First Street, Champaign, IL 61820
events.description: Join us as the Carle Illinois College of Medicine Class of 2026 takes the next step in their journey to becoming physician-innovators. On this milestone day, our students will gather with family, friends, faculty, and mentors to reveal where they will spend the next several years training in residency programs across the country.Match Day marks the culmination of years of rigorous, engineering-infused medical education, preparing our graduates to bring human-centered, technology-driven solutions to patient care. Be part of the excitement as we celebrate their achievements, honor their hard work, and send them forward to shape the future of health care.

events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Application Deadline - ILEE Dual Degree
events.description: Grainger Undergrads - Enhance your career with ILEE!  Develop an entrepreneurial mindset, enhance your career, and gain a competitive edge in the job market with the BS Dual Degree in Innovation, Leadership, and Engineering Entrepreneurship (ILEE). The ILEE degree provides knowledge, concepts, and tools to gain a competitive edge in the job market.  The application deadline for summer admission is March 31. Learn More and Apply

events.title: Art & Design Faculty Exhibition
events.description: Open Tuesday through Friday 10 am–5 pm, Saturday 10 am–4 pm, and Thursday until 8 pm when classes are in session. Accessibility information is available at: kam.illinois.edu/accessibility. Admission is always free.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: Beckman Visualization Lab Office Hours
events.description: Beckman’s Visualization Lab is open for office hours weekdays between 1-3 p.m. in 2203 Beckman, which is located just across the atrium bridge from the elevator. Manager Jay Cournoyer will be on hand to help users with the Vis Lab’s equipment and other resources for research. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? Email itg-vl@beckman.illinois.edu.

events.title: Snack & Study

Pull up a seat in our cozy Reading Room and enjoy a quiet space where you can study, read, or just take a break from it all. Free hot drinks and snacks available while supplies last!

events.title: Carr Visiting Author Series Alumni Reading: Jessica Tanck, Matthew Gavin Frank
events.description: A reading by alumni of the creative writing program, Jessica Tanck and Matthew Gavin Frank. This event is made possible by the Robert J. and Katherin Carr visiting author series.  Jessica Tanck is the author of Winter Here (UGA Press, 2024), winner of the 2022 Georgia Poetry Prize. She holds degrees from the University of Wisconsin-Madison and the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, where she completed a B.A. in English Literature - Creative Writing and Comparative Literature and an M.F.A. in Creative Writing - Poetry. Her work appears or is forthcoming in The Adroit Journal, Alaska Quarterly Review, Beloit Poetry Journal, Blackbird, Colorado Review, DIAGRAM, Gulf Coast, Kenyon Review, The Los Angeles Review, Meridian, New England Review, New Ohio Review, Ninth Letter, Waxwing, and others. Jess was born in Chicago, IL, but grew up in Sheboygan, WI, on the shores of Lake Michigan. https://www.jessicatanck.com/winter-hereMatthew Gavin Frank’s new nonfiction book, SUBMERSED: Wonder, Obsession, and Murder in the World of Amateur Submarines, will be published June 3, 2025 by Pantheon Books.  His previous nonfiction book, Flight of the Diamond Smugglers (about, among other things, the ways in which carrier pigeons were used by diamond smuggling rings in coastal South Africa) was published in 2021 by W.W. Norton: Liveright. It was selected as one of NPR's Best Books of 2021 and as a finalist for the 2021 Heartland Booksellers Award in Nonfiction. He is also the author of the nonfiction books, The Mad Feast: An Ecstatic Tour Through America’s Food (W.W. Norton: Liveright, 2015), Preparing the Ghost: An Essay Concerning the Giant Squid and Its First Photographer (W.W. Norton: Liveright, 2014), Pot Farm (The University of Nebraska Press, 2012), and Barolo (The University of Nebraska Press, 2010); the poetry books, The Morrow Plots (Black Lawrence Press, 2013), Warranty in Zulu (Barrow Street Press, 2010), and Sagittarius Agitprop (Black Lawrence Press, 2009). https://www.matthewgfrank.com/

events.title: First Tuesday Interfaith Dialogue
events.location: University YMCA, Murphy Lounge, 1001 S. Wright St., Champaign
events.description: Since 2011, the Illinois Interfaith Planning Initiative has worked to create programs designed to invite interfaith engagement, understanding, and cooperation for the University of Illinois community. Regular events include a monthly “First Tuesday” interfaith dialogue.  All people of religious and non-religious philosophical traditions are welcome!  Questions? Contact Ann Rasmus at ann@universityymca.org.

events.description: Nutrition Peers will be offering a variety of fruit to help keep students well nourished during their study hours at the Main Library. events.title: Student Racial Justice Allies & Advocates
events.description: The Racial Justice Allies and Advocates Training program is designed to provide students, faculty, and staff with a core awareness of the structures of racism, its role in interpersonal relationships, and empower allies to elevate the voices of underrepresented populations at the University of Illinois.

events.title: Champaign-Urbana AgTech Breakfast
events.location: Illinois Conference Center (1900 South First St, Champaign)
events.description: Research Park and the Champaign County Economic Development Corporation host a Champaign-Urbana AgTech Breakfast for community leaders and local professionals in the agricultural technology sector. The event is by invite-only and held 8:00-9:30am at the Illinois Conference Center. Seating is limited; if you are interested in attending, please contact Cathy McArthur at mcarthur@illinois.edu for a registration link. The university plans to capture photographs and video of this event. Be aware that if you attend, you are consenting to the possibility that your image and likeness may be shared or published by the university for marketing-communications purposes.

events.title: Player Piano Demonstration
events.location: Player Piano room on the 2nd floor of the Music & Performing Arts Library (1300 Music Building, 1114 W Nevada St)
events.description: Enjoy a free concert featuring the Music & Performing Arts Library’s Steinway Duo-Art Reproducing Piano! See the piano in action and learn more about the instrument and roll collection. This is an informal event and all are welcome! This event will take place in the Music & Performing Arts Library's player piano room, which is located on the second floor of the library. MPAL is located in the Music Building (1114 W Nevada St., Urbana). See our website for accessibility information and how to get to MPAL.

events.title: TAKEOVER
events.location: Link Gallery & School of Art & Design building
events.description: TAKEOVER is an annual extravaganza/experimental-showcase of art and design where all 180+ first year students literally takeover the bathrooms, hallways, stairwells, ceilings, galleries, and classrooms in A&D and the Link for one night only. To learn more about the TAKEOVER and to see documentation from previous years, visit this link: https://www.jorgelucero.com/work#/takeover/.

If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: Beckman Visualization Lab Office Hours
events.description: Beckman’s Visualization Lab is open for office hours weekdays between 1-3 p.m. in 2203 Beckman, which is located just across the atrium bridge from the elevator. Manager Jay Cournoyer will be on hand to help users with the Vis Lab’s equipment and other resources for research. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? Email itg-vl@beckman.illinois.edu.

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Spurlock Museum Book Club: Remember: The Journey to School Integration
events.location: The Literary, 122 N Neil St, Champaign, IL 61820
events.description: Spurlock Museum is proud to partner with The Literary (external link) to present this new community program taking place on several dates between June 2025 and April 2026. Free books will be provided to the first 13 people that sign up for participation for each date. Stop by the museum anytime during open hours to pick up your free book. Then join the group at the Literary in downtown Champaign on the designated discussion day. This book will be available for pickup at the Museum starting on March 1, 2026.

Light refreshments will be served. Registration link to follow.The university plans to capture photographs and video of this event. Be aware that if you attend, you are consenting to the possibility that your image and likeness may be shared or published by the university for marketing-communications purposes.

events.title: Beckman Visualization Lab Office Hours
events.description: Beckman’s Visualization Lab is open for office hours weekdays between 1-3 p.m. in 2203 Beckman, which is located just across the atrium bridge from the elevator. Manager Jay Cournoyer will be on hand to help users with the Vis Lab’s equipment and other resources for research. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? Email itg-vl@beckman.illinois.edu.

events.title: History Honors Colloquium

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: First Tuesday Interfaith Dialogue
events.location: University YMCA, Murphy Lounge, 1001 S. Wright St., Champaign
events.description: Since 2011, the Illinois Interfaith Planning Initiative has worked to create programs designed to invite interfaith engagement, understanding, and cooperation for the University of Illinois community. Regular events include a monthly “First Tuesday” interfaith dialogue.  All people of religious and non-religious philosophical traditions are welcome!  Contact Ann Rasmus at ann@universityymca.org.

events.title: Reading Day/IT No Change Period in effect
events.description: The Technology Services No Change period is designed to minimize the number of technical disruptions that might occur around final exams. Beginning on Reading Day and continuing a few days past the end of finals, Technology Services tries to make no technical changes to hardware or software that might disrupt the availability of the campus network, data storage, web sites or any of the software-based services that Technology Services provides to campus. Changes or updates will only be made by Technology Services for emergency purposes during the No Change period.

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Racial Justice Allies & Advocates (RJAA) staff/faculty training 2.0
events.description: The Racial Justice Allies and Advocates Training program is designed to provide students, faculty, and staff with a core awareness of the structures of racism, its role in interpersonal relationships, and empower allies to elevate the voices of underrepresented populations at the University of Illinois.

events.title: Application Deadline - Graduate Concentration in Entrepreneurship and Innovation
events.description: Grainger Graduate Students - Do you want to be a leader in your field?  Do you have an innovative or entrepreneurial spirit, or aspire to commercialize technology? Do you want to understand the role of strategic innovation in industry-relevant problems?  The Entrepreneurship & Innovation Concentration will give you the skills, resources, and experiences necessary to become a successful innovator, entrepreneur, and leader who can account for both the technical and business facets of industry-relevant problems. Plus, completion of this concentration is officially recognized on your transcript. Applications are accepted year-round, but if you want to be eligible for Summer 2026 admission, you must apply by June 1. Learn More and Apply

events.title: Champaign-Urbana AgTech Breakfast
events.location: Illinois Conference Center (1900 South First St, Champaign)
events.description: Research Park and the Champaign County Economic Development Corporation host a Champaign-Urbana AgTech Breakfast for community leaders and local professionals in the agricultural technology sector. The event is by invite-only and held 8:00-9:30am at the Illinois Conference Center. Seating is limited; if you are interested in attending, please contact Cathy McArthur at mcarthur@illinois.edu for a registration link. The university plans to capture photographs and video of this event. Be aware that if you attend, you are consenting to the possibility that your image and likeness may be shared or published by the university for marketing-communications purposes.

Manager Jay Cournoyer will be on hand to help users with the Vis Lab’s equipment and other resources for research. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? Email itg-vl@beckman.illinois.edu.

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: Application Deadline - ILEE Dual Degree
events.description: Grainger Undergrads - Enhance your career with ILEE!  Develop an entrepreneurial mindset, enhance your career, and gain a competitive edge in the job market with the BS Dual Degree in Innovation, Leadership, and Engineering Entrepreneurship (ILEE). The ILEE degree provides knowledge, concepts, and tools to gain a competitive edge in the job market.  The application deadline for fall admission is June 15. Learn More and Apply

Manager Jay Cournoyer will be on hand to help users with the Vis Lab’s equipment and other resources for research. The Vis Lab offers data visualization software, high-performance computing, 3D art services, virtual reality content creation and high-speed photography. Have questions? Email itg-vl@beckman.illinois.edu.

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. The exhibit, Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho, will be on display in the RBML through May 2025.

events.title: Innovation Studio Open Hours
events.description: Visit the Innovation Studio, a space for discovery and hands-on exploration of the latest technologies impacting teaching and learning including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, 3D printing, laser cutting, 360 photography, gaming, and more.  Strategically located in room 172 of the Armory Building, the Innovation Studio is open to everyone - students, faculty, staff, and the public - free of charge! Everyone is welcome to explore, innovate, and learn more about emerging technologies during our open hours on Mondays & Tuesdays 12-4pm.  The Innovation Studio hosts university course visits, tours, RSOs, events, research, individual consultations, and much more! If you'd like to schedule a course visit, one-on-one consultation, tour, or other event, please email us at citl-info@illinoise.edu.

events.title: 2026 Midwest Advanced MINFLUX and Super Resolution Workshop
events.location: Carl R. Woese Institute for Genomic Biology
events.description: Mark your calendar now to save July 20-24, 2026, for the 2026 Midwest Advanced MINFLUX and Super Resolution Workshop. Technology and research presentations will be Monday through Wednesday morning, with hands-on sessions in the evenings and all-day Thursday and Friday. More information is HERE.  The 7th Midwest Single-Molecule Workshop will also be that week on July 22-24 for those who are interested in attending both workshops.

events.title: Sweetbitter: The Literary Legacies and Afterlives of Sappho
events.description: The ancient poet Sappho coined “sweetbitter” in one of her most evocative verses, still striking us to the heart nearly three millennia later. But who was Sappho? As with so many ancient figures, she is more legend than person today, her story told and retold even as her poetry continues to enchant and move us. Ranging from ancient Greece to seventeenth century France to twentieth century Britain, this exhibit will explore Sappho as poet, muse, and icon. events.title: 7th Midwest Single-Molecule Workshop
events.description: The 7th Midwest Single-Molecule Workshop will bring together scientists at all career stages from across the Midwest and beyond to share cutting-edge advances in single-molecule biophysics. The workshop aims to support the growth of a vibrant single-molecule research community in the Midwest.  This two-day event will feature a dynamic program of invited lectures, trainee short talks, poster sessions, and a keynote presentation, covering both experimental and theoretical approaches applied to systems ranging from individual molecules to whole cells. Continuing the tradition of rotating among Midwestern universities, the 2026 workshop will be held at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.  The workshop will take place the same week as a MINFLUX workshop at Illinois, enabling super-resolution hands-on activities by attendees who want to participate in both.

events.title: Cat lives his best life on two legs
events.description: Every cat saved is another story to share and inspire more people to do whatever they can to help the cats in their communities. This year for International Cat Day, we’ve gathered some of our favorite cat stories from the year for your enjoyment and inspiration.

events.title: A dinosaur bigger than T. rex
events.description: A groundbreaking discovery in 2014 of a Spinosaurus with features that pointed to an aquatic lifestyle – retracted nostrils, short hind legs, a finlike tail and paddle-like feet – challenged that view.

events.title: Bumble bees are not just for killing
events.description: Mr. Geppetto is a charmer who’s beloved for his playful personality and fondness for long, snuggling naps. But despite his charm, he still was not getting picked by potential adopters visiting the Best Friends Lifesaving Center in Los Angeles.

events.title: Idaho cougars
events.description: From searching for tracks in the moonlight to letting eager hounds go on a fresh scent, running a mountain lion is a pure adrenalin rush. We only run and harvest mature mountain lions. Our professional houndsmen are great at teaching and we hope you’ll join us for this awesome sport.
